title: Foxborough files counterclaim to Patriots, Kraft Group’s lawsuit over annual stadium licensing fees
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Foxborough filed a counterclaim against Kraft Sports, seeking dismissal of the lawsuit over Gillette Stadium's licensing fees.
The town argues licensing fees are reimbursements for public safety costs, not unlawfully imposed fees as Kraft claims.
Foxborough contends the companies are contractually obligated to pay public safety costs for events at Gillette Stadium.

title: UMass Lowell soccer player killed in Ohio car crash
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: UMass Lowell soccer player Tobias Forsythe was killed in an Ohio car crash on July 5.
The crash occurred when Forsythe's car was rear-ended by a semi truck; the driver faces charges for hiding evidence.
Forsythe, a 21-year-old goalkeeper and economics major, transferred to UMass Lowell for the spring 2026 season.

title: Co-owner of Maine lumber mill dies weeks after fire, explosion that killed 2 others
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden J. Robbins, co-owner of Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, died from injuries sustained in a May 15 explosion.
The explosion and subsequent fire at Robbins Lumber also resulted in the deaths of two firefighters and injured 12 others.
The accident's cause was traced to a rapid ignition of particulate material in a silo, lifting the structure from its foundation.

title: Plymouth DA seeks assault and battery charge against Brockton mayor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth DA filed for a hearing on assault and battery charge against Brockton Mayor Moises Rodrigues.
Rodrigues allegedly inappropriately touched a 17-year-old student during a parade, leading to a formal complaint.
A clerk's hearing will determine if there's enough evidence to charge Rodrigues, but the date is unscheduled.

title: Police searching for tandem moped riders after shots fired in Roxbury
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police seek public help finding two suspects involved in Roxbury shots-fired incident on June 30.
Suspects were seen on a moped wearing black masks on Schuyler Street toward Elm Hill Avenue, Roxbury.
Police released suspect photos and urge anyone with information to contact detectives at (617) 343-4275.

title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown met with six-year-old fan Giovanni Jean at his summer program at M.I.T. after being traded to the 76ers.
Jean's tearful reaction to the trade gained over 1.1 million likes on Instagram, leading to the meeting with Brown.
The meeting included a heartfelt exchange, with Jean delivering a handwritten letter and Brown promising ongoing friendship.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was fatally shot in Cambridge early on July 4, raising criticism of ending the ShotSpotter contract.
ShotSpotter technology, deactivated in May, could have alerted responders sooner, say Cambridge police unions following Bautista's death.
Cambridge officials are hosting a community meeting to discuss the investigation and Bautista's shooting, which remains under investigation.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed his enduring love for Boston despite being traded to the 76ers from the Celtics.
Brown continues his community work in Boston through the 7uice Foundation, including launching a summer bridge program at M.I.T.
Massachusetts Governor declared July 7 as 7uice Foundation Day, honoring Brown's contributions beyond basketball.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, from Lawrence, arrested for drunk driving after crashing a Porsche on I-95 in Woburn.
Tarshi found with signs of intoxication and a forged New Hampshire RMV document in the vehicle.
The car, a Porsche 911, carried a license plate registered to a different vehicle, a Ford pickup truck.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend' on Peacock, debuting July 9, featuring a Nantucket setting.
A24's Boston-filmed 'The Drama' available on HBO Max, releasing July 31, among July's streaming highlights.
'Shark Week' content added to HBO Max from July 26, with other platforms featuring shark-themed programs.
